source.title: Reverse replay of behavioural sequences in hippocampal place cells during the awake state.

confidence_rationale: Nature paper with a well-replicated, striking finding: hippocampal place cells replay the just-completed spatial sequence in reverse at the reward location, in the awake state. This directly evidences the reward-endpoint as a trigger for hippocampal trajectory evaluation, which is the biological basis for the endpoint-PE->dopamine->BetaGate release chain in ARC-028. Confidence capped at 0.72 because the paper does not directly measure concurrent dopamine release during the reverse replay -- the dopamine coupling is inferred from reward timing and prior work, not directly shown here.
failure_signatures: The paper measures place cell firing sequences, not dopamine release. The link between reverse replay and dopamine-mediated BetaGate release (ARC-028's core claim) requires a second inferential step via reward-concurrent dopamine signalling.

Reverse replay occurs during brief awake pauses at reward locations specifically. If REE's CausalGridWorldV2 trajectories do not produce distinct reward-endpoint pauses, the biological trigger condition may not be reliably reproduced.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Immediately after spatial experience, during awake rest at the reward location, hippocampal place cells replay the sequence of positions traversed in temporally reversed order -- a signature consistent with RL-style backward trajectory evaluation.
mapping.ree_translation: ARC-028 requires the HippocampalModule to emit a trajectory completion signal at goal arrival. Foster & Wilson show that the biological analog (place cell trajectory replay) fires at the reward endpoint during waking, concurrent with dopamine signals at reward receipt. This is the waking-state mechanism for ARC-028's completion-signal -> dopamine -> BetaGate release chain: arrival at goal triggers reverse replay, which co-occurs with peak dopamine, which suppresses BG beta oscillations.
mapping.mapping_caveat: The paper's reverse replay is a sequence-evaluation mechanism; ARC-028's completion signal is an architectural trigger for BetaGate release. These are compatible but not identical: the biological system may use the same circuit for both evaluation and gate release, while REE currently separates these (HippocampalModule emits a gate signal; trajectory evaluation is implicit in the next CEM cycle). The 'reverse' structure of the replay is not directly modeled in REE's HippocampalModule.
mapping.source_context: Rodent spatial navigation task (linear track); awake replay, not sleep replay. FSCV-style place cell recording in rat hippocampus.

confidence_components.notes: Source quality high (Nature, well-replicated). Mapping fidelity penalised because the paper establishes replay-at-reward, not directly the dopamine-beta-gate chain. Transfer risk low: spatial navigation findings have good generalizability to REE's gridworld task.
